prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Frontier orbital energies (HOMO and LUMO) and their energy gap (Koopmans-style orbital gap)
Level of theory: DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: fixed single-point on the user-supplied furan geometry at /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz (no re-optimization)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software/backend: PySCF via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged (9 cycles). No engine warnings reported. Density fitting (RI) used by default. Result is for a single conformer; no thermal or conformational averaging is included. 

Headline results (all energies in eV):
  HOMO = –6.3597638 eV
  LUMO = –0.1024036 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV

The live engine log was written during the run (path echoed at job launch by chemkit; available on request) and the full JSON output is saved by chemkit at the location shown in the cli_invocation field.

The number reported under “value” is the HOMO–LUMO gap (6.2573602 eV) copied exactly from the chemkit result.
